title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a charity event.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Are you helping at the charity event on Saturday? || Man: Yes. I thought I was selling tickets, but they already have enough people for that. || Woman: So what are you doing? || Man: [8]I'll help serve tea and cakes in the afternoon, because the organisers expect lots of older visitors then[/8]. I might also carry tables before it starts. || Woman: That sounds useful. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are you helping at the charity event on Saturday?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes. I thought I was selling tickets, but they already have enough people for that.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So what are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I'll help serve tea and cakes in the afternoon, because the organisers expect lots of older visitors then. I might also carry tables before it starts.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds useful. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: What will the man mainly do at the event?
questions.explanation: The man says he will help serve tea and cakes in the afternoon.

extracts.context: You hear a woman talking about a new app.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: I've started using a new app to plan my study time. It can send reminders, but I turned those off because I already have too many alerts on my phone. || Speaker: [9]What I find useful is the weekly chart, which shows exactly how many hours I spend on each subject[/9]. It helped me see that I wasn't doing enough science. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I've started using a new app to plan my study time. It can send reminders, but I turned those off because I already have too many alerts on my phone.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: What I find useful is the weekly chart, which shows exactly how many hours I spend on each subject. It helped me see that I wasn't doing enough science. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

questions.question: What does the woman find useful about the app?
questions.explanation: She says the weekly chart showing study hours for each subject is useful.

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a holiday apartment.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you book the apartment by the sea? || Woman: Not yet. The one with the balcony was lovely, but it only had one bedroom. The cheaper one was far from the beach. || Man: What about the apartment near the old town? || Woman: [10]That's the best option, because it has two bedrooms and we can walk to restaurants in the evening[/10]. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you book the apartment by the sea?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not yet. The one with the balcony was lovely, but it only had one bedroom. The cheaper one was far from the beach.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What about the apartment near the old town?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's the best option, because it has two bedrooms and we can walk to restaurants in the evening. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: Why does the woman prefer the apartment near the old town?
questions.explanation: She says the apartment near the old town has two bedrooms, so it has enough space.

extracts.context: You hear a boy talking about a science competition.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: How did the science competition go? Did your team win? || Boy: No, we came second. The winning project was really clever, so I didn't mind. || Girl: Were you disappointed? || Boy: Not really. [11]I was pleased because our experiment worked in front of the judges, which hadn't happened when we practised at school[/11]. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: How did the science competition go? Did your team win?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: No, we came second. The winning project was really clever, so I didn't mind.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Were you disappointed?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Not really. I was pleased because our experiment worked in front of the judges, which hadn't happened when we practised at school. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: Why was the boy pleased?
questions.explanation: The boy was pleased because their experiment worked in front of the judges.

extracts.context: You hear an announcement in a shopping centre.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: Could customers please note that the lift beside the toy shop is not working this afternoon. Engineers have been called and we expect it to reopen tomorrow morning. || Speaker: [12]Customers with pushchairs should use the lift next to the car park entrance, which gives access to all floors[/12]. The stairs near the food court are still open. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Could customers please note that the lift beside the toy shop is not working this afternoon. Engineers have been called and we expect it to reopen tomorrow morning.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: Customers with pushchairs should use the lift next to the car park entrance, which gives access to all floors. The stairs near the food court are still open. Please check the information carefully and speak to a member of staff if anything is still unclear, especially if your plans have changed today.

questions.question: What should customers with pushchairs do?

options.A: use the stairs near the food court
options.B: use the lift by the car park entrance

questions.explanation: The announcement tells customers with pushchairs to use the lift next to the car park entrance.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a pet cat.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: How is your new cat getting on? || Man: She's settling in slowly. She hid under the sofa for two days, but now she walks around the flat. || Woman: Is she eating properly? || Man: Yes, but [13]she still gets frightened by the washing machine, so I close the kitchen door when it's on[/13]. Apart from that, she's fine. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How is your new cat getting on?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: She's settling in slowly. She hid under the sofa for two days, but now she walks around the flat.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Is she eating properly?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, but she still gets frightened by the washing machine, so I close the kitchen door when it's on. Apart from that, she's fine. Let's check the details again later and tell the others as well, because it will be easier if everyone knows the plan before the day.

questions.question: What problem does the cat still have?

options.C: she is scared of a machine

questions.explanation: The man says the cat still gets frightened by the washing machine.
